title: cacalia viscidula
intro: Cacalia viscidula, also known as Sticky Cacalia or Sticky Buttercup, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae), native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. Cacalia viscidula can be prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and its leaves and roots can be used in infusions, salves, or creams to harness its medicinal properties.
